trainers.name: Accompanying Flute
trainers.text: Reveal the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck. You may choose any number of Basic Pokémon you find there and put those Pokémon onto their Bench. Your opponent shuffles the other cards back into their deck.

trainers.name: Antique Cover Fossil
trainers.text:
Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic {C} Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.

At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.

trainers.name: Antique Jaw Fossil
trainers.text:
Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic {C} Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.

At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.

trainers.name: Antique Plume Fossil
trainers.text:
Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic {C} Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.

At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.

trainers.name: Antique Root Fossil
trainers.text:
Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic {C} Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.

At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.

trainers.name: Antique Sail Fossil
trainers.text:
Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic {C} Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.

At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.

trainers.name: Arven's Sandwich
trainers.text: Heal 30 damage from your Active Pokémon. If that Pokémon is an Arven's Pokémon, heal 100 damage from it instead.

trainers.name: Blowtorch
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you discard a Basic {R} Energy card from your hand.

Discard a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon, or discard a Stadium in play.

trainers.name: Boxed Order
trainers.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.

trainers.name: Buddy-Buddy Poffin
trainers.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Bug Catching Set
trainers.text: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal up to 2 in any combination of {G} Pokémon and Basic {G} Energy cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

trainers.name: Call Bell
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you go second, and only during your first turn.

Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Chill Teaser Toy
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you go second, and only during your first turn.

Put an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon into their hand.

trainers.name: Crushing Hammer
trainers.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

trainers.name: Deduction Kit
trainers.text: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back in any order, or shuffle them and put them on the bottom of your deck.

trainers.name: Dragon Elixir
trainers.text: Heal 60 damage from your Active {N} Pokémon.

trainers.name: Dusk Ball
trainers.text: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

trainers.name: Earthen Vessel
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.

Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Energy Coin
trainers.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, search your deck for a Basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Energy Recycler
trainers.text: Shuffle up to 5 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

trainers.name: Energy Retrieval
trainers.text: Put up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

trainers.name: Energy Search
trainers.text: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Energy Swatter
trainers.text: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you choose an Energy card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

trainers.name: Energy Switch
trainers.text: Move a Basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

trainers.name: Enhanced Hammer
trainers.text: Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

trainers.name: Fighting Gong
trainers.text: Search your deck for a Basic {F} Energy card or a Basic {F} Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Glass Trumpet
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you have any Tera Pokémon in play.

Choose up to 2 of your Benched {C} Pokémon and attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of them.

trainers.name: Great Haul Net
trainers.text:
Choose 1 or both:
• Shuffle up to 3 {W} Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle up to 3 Basic {W} Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

trainers.name: Hand Trimmer
trainers.text: Each player discards cards from their hand until they have 5 cards in their hand. Your opponent discards first. (If a player has 5 or fewer cards in their hand, they do not discard.)

trainers.name: Hole-Digging Shovel
trainers.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

trainers.name: Hop's Bag
trainers.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Hop's Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Iron Defender
trainers.text: During your opponent's next turn, all of your {M} Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes new Pokémon that come into play.)

trainers.name: Jumbo Ice Cream
trainers.text: Heal 80 damage from your Active Pokémon that has 3 or more Energy attached.

trainers.name: Love Ball
trainers.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon with the same name as 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Lumiose Galette
trainers.text: Heal 20 damage and remove a Special Condition from your Active Pokémon.

trainers.name: Meddling Memo
trainers.text: Your opponent counts the cards in their hand, shuffles those cards, and puts them on the bottom of their deck. If they do, they draw that many cards.

trainers.name: Mega Signal
trainers.text: Search your deck for a Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: N's PP Up
trainers.text: Attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched N's Pokémon.

trainers.name: Night Stretcher
trainers.text: Put a Pokémon or a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.

trainers.name: Ogre's Mask
trainers.text: Choose a Pokémon ex in your discard pile that has "Ogerpon" in its name, and switch it with 1 of your Pokémon ex in play that has "Ogerpon" in its name. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.

trainers.name: Poké Ball
trainers.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Poké Pad
trainers.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon that doesn't have a Rule Box, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

trainers.name: Pokégear 3.0
trainers.text: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Supporter card you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

trainers.name: Pokémon Catcher
trainers.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

trainers.name: Premium Power Pro
trainers.text: During this turn, attacks used by your {F} Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Rare Candy
trainers.text: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

trainers.name: Redeemable Ticket
trainers.text: Count your Prize cards, shuffle them, and put them on the bottom of your deck. Then, take that many cards from the top of your deck and put them face down as your Prize cards.

trainers.name: Repel
trainers.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

trainers.name: Roto-Stick
trainers.text: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. You may reveal any number of Supporter cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

trainers.name: Sacred Ash
trainers.text: Shuffle up to 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.

trainers.name: Special Red Card
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining.

Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.

trainers.name: Strange Timepiece
trainers.text: Devolve 1 of your evolved {P} Pokémon by putting any number of Evolution cards on it into your hand. (That Pokémon can't evolve this turn.)

trainers.name: Super Potion
trainers.text: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, discard an Energy from that Pokémon.

trainers.name: Switch
trainers.text: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

trainers.name: TM Machine
trainers.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards that have "Technical Machine" in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Bother-Bot
trainers.text: Turn 1 of your opponent's face-down Prize cards face up and choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card. You may have your opponent switch those cards. (That Prize card remains face up for the rest of the game.)

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Great Ball
trainers.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for an Evolution Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. If tails, search your deck for a Basic Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Transceiver
trainers.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Venture Bomb
trainers.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, put 2 damage counters on your Active Pokémon.

trainers.name: Techno Radar
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.

Search your deck for up to 2 Future Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Tera Orb
trainers.text: Search your deck for a Tera Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Tool Scrapper
trainers.text: Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to Pokémon (yours or your opponent's) and discard them.

trainers.name: Transformation Tome
trainers.text:
You must play 2 Transformation Tome cards at once. (This effect works one time for 2 cards.)

Choose a Basic Pokémon in your discard pile and switch it with 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.

trainers.name: Ultra Ball
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.

Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Wondrous Patch
trainers.text: Attach a Basic {P} Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched {P} Pokémon.

trainers.name: AZ's Tranquility
trainers.text: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you moved a Pokémon ex to your Bench in this way, heal 80 damage from that Pokémon.

trainers.name: Acerola's Mischief
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining.

Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play. During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to that Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon ex.

trainers.name: Amarys
trainers.text: Draw 4 cards. At the end of this turn, if you have 5 or more cards in your hand, discard your hand.

trainers.name: Anthea & Concordia
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you have N's Darmanitan, N's Zoroark ex, N's Vanilluxe, N's Klinklang, N's Reshiram, and N's Zekrom in play.

During this turn, if your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by your N's Pokémon, take 3 more Prize cards.

trainers.name: Bianca's Devotion
trainers.text: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has 30 HP or less remaining.

trainers.name: Billy & O'Nare
trainers.text: Draw 2 cards. Then, if you have 10 or more cards in your hand, draw 2 more cards.

trainers.name: Black Belt's Training
trainers.text: During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 40 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Boss's Orders
trainers.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

trainers.name: Briar
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if your opponent has exactly 2 Prize cards remaining.

During this turn, if your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by your Tera Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.

trainers.name: Brock's Scouting
trainers.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon or 1 Evolution Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Canari
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.

Search your deck for up to 4 {L} Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Caretaker
trainers.text: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way and if Community Center is in play, shuffle this Caretaker into your deck instead of discarding it.

trainers.name: Carmine
trainers.text:
If you go first, you may use this card during your first turn.

Discard your hand and draw 5 cards.

trainers.name: Cassiopeia
trainers.text:
You can use this card only when it is the last card in your hand.

Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Ciphermaniac's Codebreaking
trainers.text: Search your deck for 2 cards, shuffle your deck, then put those cards on top of it in any order.

trainers.name: Clemont's Quick Wit
trainers.text: Heal 60 damage from each of your {L} Pokémon.

trainers.name: Colress's Tenacity
trainers.text: Search your deck for a Stadium card and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Cook
trainers.text: Heal 70 damage from your Active Pokémon.

trainers.name: Crispin
trainers.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards of different types, reveal them, and put 1 of them into your hand. Attach the other to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Cyrano
trainers.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon ex, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Dawn
trainers.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, a Stage 1 Pokémon, and a Stage 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Drasna
trainers.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, draw 8 cards. If tails, draw 3 cards.

trainers.name: Drayton
trainers.text: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon and a Trainer card you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

trainers.name: Emcee's Hype
trainers.text: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.

trainers.name: Emma
trainers.text: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you draw a card for each Pokémon you find there.

trainers.name: Eri
trainers.text: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you discard up to 2 Item cards you find there.

trainers.name: Ethan's Adventure
trainers.text: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Ethan's Pokémon and Basic {R} Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Explorer's Guidance
trainers.text: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Discard the other cards.

trainers.name: Fennel
trainers.text: Heal 40 damage from each of your Pokémon.

trainers.name: Firebreather
trainers.text: Search your deck for up to 7 Basic {R} Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Grimsley's Move
trainers.text: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck and put a {D} Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck. You can't use this card during your first turn.

trainers.name: Harlequin
trainers.text: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards. If tails, you draw 3 cards, and your opponent draws 5 cards.

trainers.name: Hassel
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.

Look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put up to 3 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

trainers.name: Hilda
trainers.text: Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Iris's Fighting Spirit
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.

Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

trainers.name: Jacinthe
trainers.text: Heal 150 damage from 1 of your {P} Pokémon.

trainers.name: Janine's Secret Art
trainers.text: Choose up to 2 of your {D} Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic {D} Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to your Active Pokémon in this way, it is now Poisoned.

trainers.name: Jasmine's Gaze
trainers.text: During your opponent's next turn, all of your Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes new Pokémon that come into play.)

trainers.name: Judge
trainers.text: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.

trainers.name: Kieran
trainers.text:
Choose 1:

• Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

• During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex and Active Pokémon V (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Kofu
trainers.text: Put 2 cards from your hand on the bottom of your deck in any order. If you put 2 cards on the bottom of your deck in this way, draw 4 cards. (If you can't put 2 cards from your hand on the bottom of your deck, you can't use this card.)

trainers.name: Lacey
trainers.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.

trainers.name: Lana's Aid
trainers.text: Put up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box and Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

trainers.name: Larry's Skill
trainers.text: Discard your hand and search your deck for a Pokémon, a Supporter card, and a Basic Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Lillie's Determination
trainers.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.

trainers.name: Lisia's Appeal
trainers.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon to the Active Spot. If you do, the new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

trainers.name: Lt. Surge's Bargain
trainers.text: Ask your opponent if each player may take a Prize card. If yes, each player takes a Prize card. If no, you draw 4 cards.

trainers.name: Lucian
trainers.text: Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, each player flips a coin. If heads, that player draws 6 cards. If tails, they draw 3 cards.

trainers.name: Morty's Conviction
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.

Draw a card for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

trainers.name: N's Plan
trainers.text: Move up to 2 Energy from your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

trainers.name: Naveen
trainers.text: Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. Before drawing cards, you may discard any number of cards from your hand. (If you can't draw any cards in this way, you can't use this card.)

trainers.name: Perrin
trainers.text: Reveal up to 2 Pokémon in your hand and put them into your deck. If you do, search your deck for up to that many Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Philippe
trainers.text: Attach up to 2 Basic {M} Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your {M} Pokémon.

trainers.name: Pokémon Center Lady
trainers.text: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.

trainers.name: Professor Sada's Vitality
trainers.text: Choose up to 2 of your Ancient Pokémon and attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of them. If you attached any Energy in this way, draw 3 cards.

trainers.name: Professor Turo's Scenario
trainers.text: Put 1 of your Pokémon in play into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)

trainers.name: Professor's Research
trainers.text: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

trainers.name: Raifort
trainers.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and discard any number of them. Put the other cards back in any order.

trainers.name: Rosa's Encouragement
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.

Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Stage 2 Pokémon.

trainers.name: Roxie's Performance
trainers.text: During your opponent's next turn, their Poisoned Pokémon can't retreat. (This includes newly Poisoned Pokémon.)

trainers.name: Ruffian
trainers.text: Discard a Pokémon Tool and a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

trainers.name: Salvatore
trainers.text: Search your deck for a card that has no Abilities and evolves from 1 of your Pokémon, and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. You can use this card on a Pokémon you put down when you were setting up to play or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

trainers.name: Surfer
trainers.text: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.

trainers.name: Tarragon
trainers.text: Put up to 4 in any combination of {F} Pokémon and Basic {F} Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Archer
trainers.text:
You can use this card only if any of your Team Rocket's Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.

Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards.

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Ariana
trainers.text: Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. If all of your Pokémon in play are Team Rocket's Pokémon, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand instead.

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Giovanni
trainers.text: Switch your Active Team Rocket's Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Team Rocket's Pokémon. If you do, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Petrel
trainers.text: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Proton
trainers.text:
If you go first, you may use this card during your first turn.

Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

trainers.name: Tyme
trainers.text: Tell your opponent the name of a Pokémon in your hand and put that Pokémon face down in front of you. Your opponent guesses that Pokémon's HP, and then you reveal it. If your opponent guessed right, they draw 4 cards. If they guessed wrong, you draw 4 cards. Then, return the Pokémon to your hand.

trainers.name: Waitress
trainers.text: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach a Basic Energy card you find there to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

trainers.name: Wally's Compassion
trainers.text: Heal all damage from 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex. If you healed any damage in this way, put all Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand.

trainers.name: Xerosic's Machinations
trainers.text: Your opponent discards cards from their hand until they have 3 cards in their hand.

trainers.name: Academy at Night
trainers.text: Once during each player's turn, that player may put a card from their hand on top of their deck.

trainers.name: Ange Floette
trainers.text:
You can put this card into play only if you discard a Prism Tower in play, and you can put this card into play during the same turn you play Prism Tower.
Each Mega Floette ex in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets +150 HP.

trainers.name: Area Zero Underdepths
trainers.text:
Each player who has any Tera Pokémon in play can have up to 8 Pokémon on their Bench.

If a player no longer has any Tera Pokémon in play, that player discards Pokémon from their Bench until they have 5. When this card leaves play, both players discard Pokémon from their Bench until they have 5, and the player who played this card discards first.

trainers.name: Battle Cage
trainers.text: Prevent all damage counters from being placed on Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) by effects of attacks and Abilities from the opponent's Pokémon. (Damage from attacks is still taken.)

trainers.name: Community Center
trainers.text: Once during each player's turn, if they played a Supporter card from their hand this turn, they may heal 10 damage from each of their Pokémon.

trainers.name: Dizzying Valley
trainers.text: Confused Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) don't recover from that Special Condition when they evolve or devolve.

trainers.name: Festival Grounds
trainers.text: Each Pokémon that has any Energy attached (both yours and your opponent's) recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

trainers.name: Forest of Vitality
trainers.text: Each player's {G} Pokémon can evolve into {G} Pokémon during the turn they play those Pokémon, except during their first turn.

trainers.name: Full Metal Lab
trainers.text: {M} Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) take 30 less damage from attacks from the opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Granite Cave
trainers.text: Steven's Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) take 30 less damage from attacks from the opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Gravity Mountain
trainers.text: Each Stage 2 Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets -30 HP.

trainers.name: Jamming Tower
trainers.text: Pokémon Tools attached to each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) have no effect.

trainers.name: Levincia
trainers.text: Once during each player's turn, that player may put up to 2 Basic {L} Energy cards from their discard pile into their hand.

trainers.name: Lively Stadium
trainers.text: Each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets +30 HP.

trainers.name: Lumiose City
trainers.text: Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto their Bench. Then, that player shuffles their deck. If a player searches their deck in this way, their turn ends.

trainers.name: Mystery Garden
trainers.text: Once during each player's turn, that player may discard an Energy card from their hand in order to draw cards until they have as many cards in their hand as they have {P} Pokémon in play.

trainers.name: N's Castle
trainers.text: N's Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Retreat Cost.

trainers.name: Nighttime Mine
trainers.text: Attacks used by each Tera Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) cost {C} more.

trainers.name: Perilous Jungle
trainers.text: During Pokémon Checkup, put 2 more damage counters on each Poisoned non-{D} Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

trainers.name: Postwick
trainers.text: Attacks used by Hop's Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) do 30 more damage to the opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Prism Tower
trainers.text: Once during each player's turn, that player may discard 2 cards from their hand in order to draw a card.

trainers.name: Risky Ruins
trainers.text: Whenever any player puts a Basic non-{D} Pokémon onto their Bench during their turn, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

trainers.name: Spikemuth Gym
trainers.text: Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for a Marnie's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.

trainers.name: Surfing Beach
trainers.text: Once during each player's turn, that player may switch their Active {W} Pokémon with 1 of their Benched {W} Pokémon.

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Factory
trainers.text: Once during each player's turn, if they played a Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name from their hand this turn, they may draw 2 cards.

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Watchtower
trainers.text: {C} Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.

trainers.name: Adversity Policy
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has Weakness to your opponent's Active Pokémon's type, is in the Active Spot, and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), draw 3 cards.

trainers.name: Air Balloon
trainers.text: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is {C}{C} less.

trainers.name: Ancient Booster Energy Capsule
trainers.text: The Ancient Pokémon this card is attached to gets +60 HP, recovers from all Special Conditions, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

trainers.name: Babiri Berry
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent's {M} Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.

trainers.name: Binding Mochi
trainers.text: Attacks used by the Poisoned Pokémon this card is attached to do 40 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Brave Bangle
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to doesn't have a Rule Box, the attacks it uses do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

trainers.name: Colbur Berry
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent's {D} Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.

trainers.name: Core Memory
trainers.text: The Mega Zygarde ex this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)

trainers.name: Counter Gain
trainers.text: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, attacks used by the Pokémon this card is attached to cost {C} less.

trainers.name: Cynthia's Power Weight
trainers.text: The Cynthia's Pokémon this card is attached to gets +70 HP.

trainers.name: Future Booster Energy Capsule
trainers.text: The Future Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost, and the attacks it uses do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Gravity Gemstone
trainers.text: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, the Retreat Cost of both Active Pokémon is {C} more.

trainers.name: Haban Berry
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent's {N} Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.

trainers.name: Handheld Fan
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), move an Energy from the Attacking Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

trainers.name: Heavy Baton
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has a Retreat Cost of exactly 4, is in the Active Spot, and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move up to 3 Basic Energy cards from that Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

trainers.name: Hop's Choice Band
trainers.text: Attacks used by the Hop's Pokémon this card is attached to cost {C} less and do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Light Ball
trainers.text: Attacks used by the Pikachu ex this card is attached to do 50 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Lillie's Pearl
trainers.text: If the Lillie's Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card.

trainers.name: Lucky Helmet
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), draw 2 cards.

trainers.name: Occa Berry
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent's {R} Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.

trainers.name: Passho Berry
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent's {W} Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.

trainers.name: Payapa Berry
trainers.text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent's {P} Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.

trainers.name: Powerglass
trainers.text: At the end of your turn (after your attack), if the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to it.

trainers.name: Punk Helmet
trainers.text: If the {D} Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 4 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

trainers.name: Rescue Board
trainers.text: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is {C} less. If that Pokémon's remaining HP is 30 or less, it has no Retreat Cost.

trainers.name: Sacred Charm
trainers.text: The Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have an Ability (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

trainers.name: Team Rocket's Hypnotizer
trainers.text: If the Team Rocket's Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Team Rocket's Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Asleep.

trainers.name: Technical Machine: Fluorite
trainers.text: The Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.) If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.

trainers.name: Thick Scale
trainers.text: The {N} Pokémon this card is attached to takes 50 less damage from attacks from your opponent's {G}, {R}, {W}, or {L} Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
